channel.title: What Is Being Built — Daily Activity Feed
channel.description: Daily updates on open-source automated research tools: commits, releases, and community activity.

item.title: Daily digest — 2026-06-25
item.description:
Our community has seen exciting progress across multiple projects. The **giladfeldman/docpluck** and **giladfeldman/escicheck** repositories have both received updates to their 'main' branches, with docpluck adding a new feature for table detection and escicheck syncing with the latest changes from effectcheck versions 0.6.4, 0.6.5, and 0.6.6. Meanwhile, **shaheedazaad/auto-zcurve** has revised its validation note to include a citation, and **giovannacdelsordo/output-it-forward** has added a convenient Chrome Webstore link. These updates demonstrate the connections between our projects, with escicheck's syncs highlighting the importance of collaboration and version control. Overall, it's great to see our community's projects continuing to evolve and improve, with a focus on documentation, validation, and user accessibility.

64 commits across 5 active repositories this week.

Dormant repos revived: escicheck

Most active:

regcheck17 commits
docpluck16 commits
coarse15 commits
metacheck13 commits
escicheck3 commits

item.title: metacheck: 4 commits today
item.description:
scienceverse/metacheck

The main branch saw changes to the home page and navigation bar, with the text on the home page being updated to "Point home page text to the Metacheck Manual" and the navbar being modified to replace "Get Started/Reference/Articles" with a "Metacheck Manual link". On the dev branch, a fix was applied to address the "coi_check/funding_check cold-start failure", and a new file "R/import-papers.R" was added to ensure that dev installs cleanly. The dev branch also saw the merger of several pull requests, including "#317 from scienceverse/papers_databases", "#316 from scienceverse/ethics_check", "#309 from scienceverse/prereg_regcheck", and "#318 from scienceverse/fix/code-check-paperlist", which were merged by Lisa DeBruine. Both the main and dev branches were worked on in these commits.

4 commit(s) today:

  • 32659a9 [main] Point home page text to the Metacheck Manual — Lakens
  • d6a7f12 [main] Replace Get Started/Reference/Articles navbar with Metacheck Manual link — Lakens

View all commits →

item.title: docpluck: 3 commits today
item.description:
giladfeldman/docpluck

Work was done on the 'main' branch, where a feature was added for tables, specifically an "RC-T char-level column-detection fallback for tight-kerned tables" as part of version 2.4.98, which is described as the foundation. The documentation was also updated on the 'main' branch, with the addition of "2026-06-25 run handoff + LEARNINGS + project lessons", although this is noted as being partial. Additionally, on the 'main' branch, a fix was made to the tooling, which included adding a "direction-aware check_app_pin_sync note" and incorporating "RC-T/RC-1 spec findings". No other branches were worked on in these commits. The changes are specific to the 'main' branch, with no other branches mentioned.

3 commit(s) today:

  • 521089a [main] feat(tables): RC-T char-level column-detection fallback for tight-kerned tables (v2.4.98, foundation) — Gilad Feldman
  • 976af30 [main] docs(iterate): 2026-06-25 run handoff + LEARNINGS + project lessons (PARTIAL) — Gilad Feldman
  • 59cff5b [main] fix(tooling): direction-aware check_app_pin_sync note + RC-T/RC-1 spec findings — Gilad Feldman

View all commits →

item.title: regcheck: 2 commits today
item.description:
JamieCummins/regcheck

The staging branch was the focus of the recent work, where a "debug of CLI report" was performed. Additionally, on the staging branch, "further local LLM support in CLI" was added, enhancing the command-line interface's capabilities. The staging branch also received "UI fixes", which addressed issues with the user interface. The commits all occurred on the same day, indicating a concentrated effort to refine the project's functionality and usability on the staging branch.

2 commit(s) today:

  • b0d8ab2 [staging] debug of CLI report — Jamie Cummins
  • 97e8203 [staging] add further local LLM support in CLI — Jamie Cummins
  • df873ec [staging] UI fixes — Jamie Cummins
  • 5b53ad6 [staging] fix OSF + mutlistudy bugs — Jamie Cummins
  • 71be3be [staging] harden security features — Jamie Cummins
  • fbd5030 [staging] firm up qwen formatting — Jamie Cummins
  • f775856 [staging] improve qwen — Jamie Cummins
  • 724d020 [staging] re-add qwen! — Jamie Cummins
  • 89a1470 [staging] push for redeploy — Jamie Cummins
  • baebc81 [staging] hunting down osf bug — Jamie Cummins
  • 2f5eaaa [staging] improve logging — Jamie Cummins
  • 7fb80fe [staging] improve error message — Jamie Cummins
  • a2f7302 [staging] fix osf bug — Jamie Cummins
  • d2089d5 [staging] remove groq endpoints — Jamie Cummins
  • 51b9ba6 [staging] harden json handling — Jamie Cummins
  • 6cdde08 [staging] fix oss bug — Jamie Cummins
  • 1fd2de7 [staging] use more efficient storage — Jamie Cummins
  • 4b7a527 [staging] patch oss SDK handler — Jamie Cummins
  • e514303 [staging] fix oss handling bug — Jamie Cummins
  • 599489b [staging] harden qwen handling — Jamie Cummins

View all commits →

item.title: escicheck: 1 commit today
item.description:
giladfeldman/escicheck

The main branch was the focus of the recent work, with three commits made to it. The most recent commit, made on June 25, "sync from escicheckapp@f37b5a1 (effectcheck v0.6.6)" as stated, which suggests that the repository was synchronized with the escicheckapp repository at the specified commit hash, bringing in changes from effectcheck version 0.6.6. On June 21, two commits were made to the main branch, one to "sync from escicheckapp@bd6f007 (effectcheck v0.6.5)" and another to "sync from escicheckapp@dec37b1 (effectcheck v0.6.4)", indicating that the repository was synchronized with the escicheckapp repository at those specific points, incorporating changes from effectcheck versions 0.6.5 and 0.6.4, respectively. No other branches were worked on during this period, with all changes being made to the main branch.

1 commit(s) today:

  • 244b2d8 [main] sync from escicheckapp@f37b5a1 (effectcheck v0.6.6) (#20) — Gilad Feldman

⚠ This repository was dormant and has recently become active again.

View all commits →
